question: How effective are pharmacists at providing vaccinations compared to doctors?
topic: Pharmacy-based vaccination and public health access
ground_truth_data_points: Pharmacies administered 48% of adult flu vaccines in 2023-24 season (CDC RespVaxView)
71.5% of updated COVID-19 vaccines given at pharmacies vs 9.2% at doctor offices (CDC)
Pharmacist-administered vaccination increases uptake by 14% (RR 1.14, meta-analysis of 14 RCTs + 79 observational studies)
All 50 states authorize pharmacists to administer vaccines
Federal Retail Pharmacy Program delivered 67.7% of bivalent COVID doses (40.5M of 59.8M)

sources.name: CDC RespVaxView — Vaccination Settings Report 2023-24
sources.note: Pharmacies are dominant adult vaccination site: flu 48%, COVID 71.5%, RSV 81.7%

sources.name: CDC MMWR — Federal Retail Pharmacy Program COVID-19 Report
sources.note: 67.7% of bivalent COVID doses via pharmacies, equity data by demographics

sources.name: Le et al. (2022) — Pharmacist Impact on Immunization Uptake: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
sources.note: 14 RCTs + 79 observational studies. Pharmacist immunizers RR 1.14, advocates RR 1.31

sources.name: Abd Rahim et al. (2023) — Pharmacist Interventions on Immunisation Uptake: Systematic Review
sources.note: 19 studies, pharmacist interventions improved uptake by up to 51% (RR 1.51)

sources.name: NASEM (2025) — Innovations in Pharmacy Training and Sustainable Practice
sources.note: NASEM workshop on pharmacist expanding role, pharmacy deserts, workforce

sources.name: NASPA — State Pharmacist Vaccination Authority (2025)
sources.note: All 50 states authorize pharmacist vaccination, varying scope, PREP Act through 2029
